needs_ken.question: Should the CREST v1.3 judgment benchmark (current model gemma4:31b-cloud) be re-tested on gemma4:26b as a quality regression check before adoption?
needs_ken.context: The benchmark currently scores 20/20 on gemma4:31b-cloud. gemma4:26b is faster (58.6 vs ~40 t/s for 31b) but a smaller MoE (25.8B total, 8 of 128 experts). Whether 26b clears the >=90% threshold for this Verify-judge task is untested.
needs_ken.decision_left: Script kept at gemma4:31b-cloud for CHG-0963. Forge did NOT change the model in this round.
needs_ken.recommendation: Run scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh with MODEL=gemma4:26b as a one-off ad-hoc test and compare to current 20/20 baseline. If 26b also clears >=90%, swap the script default. Otherwise keep 31b.

metadata_update.rollback_plan: 1. Restore state/model-policy.json from git/backup (re-add 'ollama/gemma4:26b' to globalProhibitedInInteractive; remove directCallLocalModels section). 2. Revert scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh and scripts/context-summarize.sh comment additions (no model changes were made, so script behavior is unchanged). 3. openclaw.json was not touched — no rollback needed.
